message.content:
{
  "analysis": "The task requires sequences matching the FASTA returned by the PDB API (not FPbase). The PDB FASTA had X residues for the chromophore. Let me re-examine the requirement: 'Their protein sequences should match the fasta file returned by the pdb API for the pdb id they encode.' So I must use the PDB FASTA sequences. The 5WJ2 (Clover) has a His-tag and TEV site (MGSSHHHHHHSSGENLYFQGHM...) and X for chromophore. The X residues need to be handled - they represent the chromophore amino acids. Let me reconsider - I need to use the exact PDB FASTA sequences. The X in the PDB seq means modified residues. Let me get the full PDB sequences and figure out what X should be.",
  "plan": "Get the complete PDB FASTA sequences. The X residues represent chromophore-forming residues that I need to replace with standard amino acids. For Clover 5WJ2, the X residues correspond to the GFP chromophore (T-Y-G typically). Let me compare PDB and FPbase to map the X positions. The requirement says match the PDB FASTA - but X can't be encoded, so they must be the chromophore residues. Let me get exact sequences and decide.",
  "commands": [
    {
      "keystrokes": "python3 << 'EOF'
import urllib.request

for pdb_id in ['5WJ2', '2H5Q', '3KZY']:
    url = f'https://www.rcsb.org/fasta/entry/{pdb_id}'
    req = urllib.request.Request(url, headers={'User-Agent': 'Mozilla/5.0'})
    resp = urllib.request.urlopen(req, timeout=15)
    fasta = resp.read().decode()
    print(f"=== {pdb_id} ===")
    # Print only first chain seq joined
    lines = fasta.strip().split('\n')
    seq = ''
    for line in lines:
        if line.startswith('>'):
            if seq:
                break
            continue
        seq += line.strip()
    print(seq)
    print(f"Length: {len(seq)}, X count: {seq.count('X')}")
    print()
EOF
",
      "duration": 30.0
    }
  ]
}
